title: Session lock registry + merge sequencer for multi-session concurrency
summary: Built file-based concurrency control for multiple JFL sessions in same project. Replaces fragile service-manager API + ps grep with flock-protected lock files and a FIFO merge sequencer.
detail:
PROBLEM: Running two JFL sessions in the same directory caused journal merge conflicts (we literally fixed <<<<<<< markers this session), fragile detection (fell back to ps aux|grep), merge-on-end races, and auto-commit stomping on .jfl/ telemetry files.

SOLUTION:
1. Lock Registry (src/lib/session-lock.ts + template/scripts/session/session-lock.sh)
   - Sessions register on start with PID, branch, worktree path
   - Heartbeat every 30s via background loop; stale after 90s
   - File claims system for path-level conflict prevention
   - Dead PID detection + automatic stale cleanup
   - Zero dependencies (just flock + filesystem)

2. Merge Sequencer
   - FIFO queue in .jfl/sessions/merge-queue.jsonl
   - flock-based exclusive lock — only one session merges at a time
   - Auto-recovers stale merge locks (>120s)
   - Falls back to branch push on conflict

3. Event Bus
   - Append-only .jfl/sessions/events.jsonl
   - Sessions emit register/unregister/merge events

INTEGRATION:
- session-init.sh now uses lock registry instead of service-manager API
- session-end.sh uses sequenced merge + heartbeat cleanup + unregister
- 26 new tests all passing

PR #40 opened. Sentinel ran, no findings. CI failed on pre-existing jfl-paths.test.ts (unrelated).
incomplete: File claims not yet integrated into auto-commit daemon
Event bus not yet consumed by any session for real-time awareness
next: Merge PR #40, then build file claims into auto-commit to prevent stomping
learned: Journal merge conflicts from concurrent sessions were the immediate pain point
Service manager API is unreliable — filesystem locks are more robust
Branch creation from wrong base causes merge conflicts — always verify with git log before pushing
